title: crematomia andrieuxii
intro: Crematomia andrieuxii, also known as the New Zealand mistletoe or kawakawa mistletoe, is a parasitic plant that grows on the host plant Kawakawa (Macropiper excelsum) in New Zealand. It has been used in traditional Maori medicine for centuries, valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antifungal activities. The plant has been used to treat various health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, as well as to promote wound healing and reduce pain. Herbal preparations of Crematomia andrieuxii include infusions, tinctures, and powders, which can be used topically or internally to harness its medicinal benefits.
